first_paragraph: ***Woman to Woman*** is a 1923 British [silent](/wiki/Silent_film "Silent film") [drama film](/wiki/Drama_film "Drama film") directed by [Graham Cutts](/wiki/Graham_Cutts "Graham Cutts"), with [Alfred Hitchcock](/wiki/Alfred_Hitchcock "Alfred Hitchcock") as the uncredited assistant director and co-screenwriter.[[2]](#cite_note-HitchcockGuide-2)[[3]](#cite_note-silentera-3) The film was the first of three adaptions of the 1921 play *[Woman to Woman](/wiki/Woman_to_Woman_(play) "Woman to Woman (play)")* by [Michael Morton](/wiki/Michael_Morton_(dramatist) "Michael Morton (dramatist)"). To capitalise on the success of the film, Cutts and Hitchcock made another film, *[The White Shadow](/wiki/The_White_Shadow_(film) "The White Shadow (film)")*, with Compson before she returned to the United States.[[2]](#cite_note-HitchcockGuide-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1923 film

| Written by | Graham Cutts Alfred Hitchcock (uncredited) |

| Produced by | [Michael Balcon](/wiki/Michael_Balcon "Michael Balcon") [Victor Saville](/wiki/Victor_Saville "Victor Saville") |
| Starring | [Betty Compson](/wiki/Betty_Compson "Betty Compson") |
| Cinematography | Claude McDonnell |
| Edited by | [Alma Reville](/wiki/Alma_Reville "Alma Reville") |
| Distributed by | [Woolf & Freedman Film Service](/wiki/Woolf_%26_Freedman_Film_Service "Woolf & Freedman Film Service") |
| Release date | * August 1923 (1923-08) |
| Running time | 82 minutes (8 [reels](/wiki/Film_reel "Film reel"); 7455 feet) |
| Country | United Kingdom |

| Budget | more than £40,000[[1]](#cite_note-michael-1) |

***Woman to Woman*** is a 1923 British [silent](/wiki/Silent_film "Silent film") [drama film](/wiki/Drama_film "Drama film") directed by [Graham Cutts](/wiki/Graham_Cutts "Graham Cutts"), with [Alfred Hitchcock](/wiki/Alfred_Hitchcock "Alfred Hitchcock") as the uncredited assistant director and co-screenwriter.[[2]](#cite_note-HitchcockGuide-2)[[3]](#cite_note-silentera-3) The film was the first of three adaptions of the 1921 play *[Woman to Woman](/wiki/Woman_to_Woman_(play) "Woman to Woman (play)")* by [Michael Morton](/wiki/Michael_Morton_(dramatist) "Michael Morton (dramatist)"). To capitalise on the success of the film, Cutts and Hitchcock made another film, *[The White Shadow](/wiki/The_White_Shadow_(film) "The White Shadow (film)")*, with Compson before she returned to the United States.[[2]](#cite_note-HitchcockGuide-2)

Hitchcock met his future wife, [Alma Reville](/wiki/Alma_Reville "Alma Reville"), while working on this film.[[4]](#cite_note-BFI75-4)

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Woman_to_Woman_(1923_film)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

As described in a film magazine review,[[5]](#cite_note-5) Deloryse, a dancer of exquisite charm and grace, is wooed and won by David Compton, an English officer billeted in Paris. On the eve of their marriage, her fiancée is unexpectedly called away. A blow to the head robs him of his memory and he forgets all about the faithful young woman who sacrificed all for him. Later, fate brings them together and, while the man's heart is wrung by the wrong that he has unwittingly done to Deloryse by marrying another woman, Deloryse's one thought is to protect the future of their son. For this, she sacrifices herself by dancing at a fete of the second woman in the case, even after a doctor had warned her that to do so would be fatal.

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Woman_to_Woman_(1923_film)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Betty Compson](/wiki/Betty_Compson "Betty Compson") as Louise Boucher / Deloryse
* [Clive Brook](/wiki/Clive_Brook "Clive Brook") as David Compton / David Anson-Pond
* [Josephine Earle](/wiki/Josephine_Earle "Josephine Earle") as Mrs. Anson-Pond
* [Marie Ault](/wiki/Marie_Ault "Marie Ault") as Henrietta
* Myrtle Peter as Davy
* [A. Harding Steerman](/wiki/A._Harding_Steerman "A. Harding Steerman") as Doctor
* [Tom Coventry](/wiki/Tom_Coventry "Tom Coventry")
* [Aubrey Fitzgerald](/wiki/Aubrey_Fitzgerald "Aubrey Fitzgerald")
* Donald Searle
* [Madge Tree](/wiki/Madge_Tree "Madge Tree")
* George Turner
* [Henry Vibart](/wiki/Henry_Vibart "Henry Vibart")
* [Victor McLaglen](/wiki/Victor_McLaglen "Victor McLaglen") as Nubian Slave (uncredited)

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Woman_to_Woman_(1923_film)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Production")]

Michael Balcon and Victor Saville established a new film company. They were approached by Graham Cutts who had optioned film rights to the play. The producers raised the budget from a variety of sources including £7,000 C.W. Woolf and from Oscar Deutsch. Betty Compson was imported from the US at £1,000 a week on a two picture deal.[[1]](#cite_note-michael-1)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Woman_to_Woman_(1923_film)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Reception")]

The film was a big commercial success but the second film with Compson, *The White Shadow* as a flop.[[1]](#cite_note-michael-1)

## Preservation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Woman_to_Woman_(1923_film)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Preservation")]

As of August 2010, the film is missing from the [BFI National Archive](/wiki/BFI_National_Archive "BFI National Archive"), and is listed as one of the [British Film Institute](/wiki/British_Film_Institute "British Film Institute")'s "[75 Most Wanted](/wiki/BFI_75_Most_Wanted "BFI 75 Most Wanted")" [lost films](/wiki/Lost_film "Lost film").[[4]](#cite_note-BFI75-4)
